definitions.text: By or for the cause that; on this account that; for the reason that.
examples: I hid myself because I was afraid.

definitions.text: As is known, inferred, or determined from the fact that.
examples: It must be broken, because I pressed the button and nothing happened.
I don't think he is a nice person, because he yells at people for no reason.
My life is ruined because of you!

definitions.text: Used alone to refuse to provide a full answer to a question begun with "why", usually taken as an anapodoton of the elided full phrase "Because I said so".

examples: He rejected me because revenge, I guess.
